other_observers: Brenda Linfield Cyndi Mosch; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been posted on cobirds.
description: The bird was generally similar in size and shape to an American Coot.  It had yellowish legs, with very long, completely unwebbed toes.  It had deep purple underparts; a dull, dark, olive green back; and unmarked and lighter iridescent purple sides.  It had white undertail coverts.  It had a red bill with a yellow tip.  The frontal shield was pale blue, with a worn appearance.Behaviors: It was up in a conifer tree when I first saw it! Later, it flew down onto a lawn. This is obviously not typical behavior for this species.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
